label: USG Abdomen — May 2026
facility: Aggarwal Digital X-Ray, 3D/4D USG, Colour Doppler Centre, Ambala City
doctor: Dr. Manisha Aggarwal (MBBS DNB Radiology, MNAMS)
summary: USG Abdomen reveals Grade-2 Fatty Liver with enlarged liver (19 cm span) and Mild Prostatomegaly Grade-1 (prostate volume 27 cc). All other organs — gallbladder, pancreas, spleen, kidneys, and urinary bladder — are normal. No hydronephrosis, no calculus, no free fluid.

sections.title: Liver
sections.note: Liver is enlarged at 19 cm (normal 13–16 cm) with Grade-2 fatty changes. This is consistent with uncontrolled diabetes (HbA1c 12.7%), obesity, and high triglycerides (238 mg/dL). Grade-2 means moderate fat accumulation — often improvable, and sometimes reversible, with gradual weight loss, blood sugar control, lipid control, smoking cessation, zero alcohol, and clinician follow-up.

sections.title: Gallbladder & Biliary System
sections.note: Gallbladder is normal on this scan — no stones, no wall thickening, no sludge. This makes gallbladder disease much less likely for the 7 May 2026 pain episode, but persistent or recurrent severe RUQ pain still needs clinical review.

sections.title: Kidneys
sections.note: Both kidneys are structurally normal with no stones, no swelling (hydronephrosis), and good corticomedullary differentiation. This is reassuring given the hyperfiltration (eGFR 118) noted in the May 2 blood report. However, structural normality does not rule out early diabetic nephropathy — a urine microalbumin test is still recommended.

sections.title: Prostate
sections.note: Prostate is mildly enlarged at 27 cc (normal ~20–25 cc for this age). This is very common in men over 50 and is not alarming. Grade-1 means the mildest form. Only needs attention if urinary symptoms develop (weak stream, frequent nighttime urination, hesitancy). No treatment needed unless symptomatic.

sections.title: Other Findings — All Clear
items: Pancreas & Spleen — Normal in size, outline & echotexture. No focal lesion.
Urinary Bladder — Normal outline & wall thickness with clear (anechoic) lumen.
Free Fluid — No free fluid seen in abdomen (reassuring on this scan).
Retroperitoneal Lymph Nodes — No upper RP LAP (lymphadenopathy) seen.
Right Iliac Fossa — No dilated tubular aperistaltic structure seen (appendicitis not seen on this scan).
sections.note: All remaining organs and areas are normal. Importantly, no signs of appendicitis or intra-abdominal fluid collection.

sections.title: Connection to Existing Conditions
items: Fatty Liver ← Uncontrolled Diabetes — HbA1c 12.7% drives insulin resistance, which increases liver fat deposition. Controlling blood sugar is the #1 intervention.
Fatty Liver ← Dyslipidemia — Triglycerides 238 mg/dL and LDL 181.7 mg/dL contribute directly to hepatic fat accumulation.
Fatty Liver ← Obesity — The report itself notes 'poor acoustic window due to obesity.' Visceral fat is a primary driver of liver steatosis.
Enlarged Liver (19 cm) — Hepatomegaly is expected with Grade-2 fatty infiltration. The liver swells as fat accumulates in hepatocytes.
Kidneys Normal — Reassuring given the eGFR hyperfiltration (118) from blood work. Structural health is preserved for now.
Gallbladder Normal — Despite high triglycerides (a risk factor for gallstones), no stones or sludge were found.
Prostate Mild Enlargement — Age-related BPH (Benign Prostatic Hyperplasia). Common at 53. Monitor for urinary symptoms.
sections.note: The USG confirms that the metabolic syndrome (diabetes + dyslipidemia + obesity) is affecting the liver. Grade-2 fatty liver is often improvable and sometimes reversible with sustained lifestyle, blood sugar, lipid, weight, and smoking-cessation work; fibrosis risk still needs LFT/CBC-based staging such as FIB-4.

consultationPlan.note: Grade-2 fatty liver needs baseline LFTs (ALT, AST, GGT) and follow-up plan to prevent progression to NASH/fibrosis.
Fatty liver is directly linked to uncontrolled HbA1c. Intensify glycemic control.
Mild prostatomegaly Grade-1 — only if urinary symptoms (weak stream, nocturia, hesitancy) develop.

impression: Fatty Liver (Grade-2) — Moderate steatosis, often improvable with sustained intervention
Mild Prostatomegaly Grade-1 — Age-related, monitor for urinary symptoms
limitationsNoted: Poor acoustic window due to obesity, bowel gases & body habitus — high-up position of structures. Some deeper structures may not be fully visualized.
